political.s: Via parent company Nestlé: FEC: $11K PAC donations; 59% to Republican, 41% to Democratic committees.

charity.s: $220M reported corporate giving in 2024 (~0.19% of revenue) via Nestle Healthcare Nutrition.

environment.s: Via parent company Nestlé: Bonsucro member with majority-certified sugarcane volume. [Bonsucro member]

dei.s: Via parent company Nestlé: Nestlé maintains active DEI programs including a Gender Balance Acceleration Plan, ILO Global Business Disability Network (GBDN) Charter signatory (signed 2024), Partnership with Global LGBTI Equality (PGLE) membership, UN Women's Empowerment Principles signatory, and a 2025 Global Equal Pay review reporting no countrywide pay gap across 84 countries. However, in March 2025 the company was targeted by a consumer boycott ('Nestlé Blackout,' March 21–28, 2025) organized by The People's Union USA, alleging Nestlé had scaled back DEI commitments—though no formal regulatory finding of DEI policy violation has been issued.

animals.s: Via parent company Nestlé: BBFAW 2024 Tier 3 — group-wide farm-animal welfare policy with audited annual progress. CIWF Good Chicken Award (Europe) + Good Egg Award (global). [BBFAW Tier 3 · CIWF Award]

privacy.s: Via parent company Nestlé: Nestlé operates a mandatory Global Privacy Program aligned with GDPR Binding Corporate Rules (updated January 2025) and employs a worldwide Nestlé Privacy Network. However, the company experienced two publicly claimed data incidents: in March 2022, hacktivist group Anonymous claimed to have leaked 10 GB of Nestlé data (emails, passwords, client data), which Nestlé denied; and in April 2024, threat actor group R00TK1T claimed responsibility for infiltrating Nestlé's systems and leaking confidential internal data—Nestlé had not issued a formal public confirmation of the 2024 breach as of reporting. No regulatory fines against Nestlé specifically for privacy violations have been found in public records.

wiki.extract: S.Pellegrino is an Italian natural mineral water and drinks brand, owned by the company Sanpellegrino S.p.A., part of Swiss company Nestlé since 1997. The principal production plant is located in San Pellegrino Terme in the province of Bergamo, Lombardy, Italy. Its products are exported worldwide.

health.s: Via parent company Nestlé: 4 CPSC recalls on record; sample hazard: if microwaved or filled with extremely hot liquid.
